title: Tenderly Charred Irish Lamb Tagine
description: A charred, crisp, and festive Irish dish featuring phyllo dough and cooked in a tajine clay vessel, this recipe is perfect for special occasions. It is also sugar-free-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb pieces with salt, black pepper, and dried thyme.
Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a tajine clay vessel over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb pieces until browned on all sides, then remove from the vessel and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the vessel and cook until it is lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic to the vessel and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth to the vessel and bring to a boil.
Return the lamb pieces to the vessel, cover with phyllo dough, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the phyllo dough is golden brown.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
